public_claim: The logistics establish a long-running routine at one building, not the identity of the person behind it. Separately, unusual Meridian options activity shortly before the disappearance creates a profit trail worth investigating, but it does not yet prove a crime or victim.

private_messages.message: I need a bounded account of what your data directly shows: timestamps, accounts, devices, and transfers—not conclusions about who R. was. Preserve the originals and tell me what you can provide voluntarily; I am making no promises beyond lawful handling.

Markets flagged unusual options activity against Meridian in the hours before R. went dark. State your positions, timing, information source, and who else knew. Profit is motive evidence, not guilt, but concealment will change how I treat it.

ability: Neither Compel nor Narrow Immunity Offer used; both reserved pending identification of a specific document holder or a witness admitting a specific offense tied to a personally observed act.
goal_progress: Separated proof of routine from proof of identity or harm.
Opened a voluntary evidence channel with Wren while avoiding overbroad promises.
Put Grayson on notice regarding the documented profit trail.
Reserved coercive tools until their targets and evidentiary value are concrete.
reasoning_summary: The delivery trail proves coordinated activity at a building but cannot identify who ordered or collected the food. M03 makes financial benefit a productive lead, especially concerning Grayson, while Wren is the best route to timestamps and account-level evidence. With no body, verified identity, or observed harm, crime remains plausible but unproven, and any victim should not yet be labeled R.
